The torso is longer than previous years, accommodating female body proportions without excess baggy fabric at the hips.

Verdict: Worth Buying in 2026?

Yes. The ION Amaze Element women's wetsuit is a genuine investment in comfort and performance. The 2026 updates—recycled neoprene, refined seams, and improved hood fit—show ION is listening to real users. For women serious about water sports in European waters, this suit delivers on warmth, mobility, and durability without the premium price tag of ultra-luxury alternatives.

The sealed and taped construction means fewer leaks and longer effective lifespan. The ION Amaze Element women's wetsuit will maintain thermal integrity for 3–4 seasons of regular use. The revised flatlock seams are less irritating, and the fit accommodates athletic female builds far better than mainstream unisex designs. If you spend winter months on the water and value unrestricted movement, this is the right suit.

Rating: 9/10 – Exceptional warmth-to-weight ratio, natural fit, and proven durability. Minor point deduction only for hood attachment that can still bunch in extreme wind chop.

Find the ION Amaze Element women's wetsuit at Surf Store. Our team are water sports athletes too—we'll help you choose the right size and answer any questions about fit.

title: ION Base 3/2 Wetsuit 2026 — Budget Wetsuit Review
meta_title: ION Base 3/2 Wetsuit 2026 Review | Surf Store
meta_description: Expert review of the ION Base 3/2 wetsuit for 2026. Warmth, flex, and durability tested. Find it at Surf Store.
excerpt: The ION Base 3/2 delivers reliable warmth and movement freedom for autumn and spring water sports across Europe.
body_html:
What's New for 2026

ION has refined the Base 3/2 wetsuit for 2026 with improved seam tape coverage and a revised chest panel that reduces water flushing during dynamic movements. The neoprene blend now features a higher percentage of limestone-derived material, making the suit more eco-conscious without compromising stretch or thermal retention. The back zip has been upgraded with a smoother slider mechanism for easier entry and exit—a small change that makes a real difference on cold mornings.

Key Features & Technology

At its core, the ION Base 3/2 wetsuit uses ION's proprietary Superstretch neoprene, which offers exceptional mobility compared to standard rubber compounds. The 3mm chest and arms paired with 2mm legs strike a practical balance: you stay warm where you need it most, whilst maintaining leg mobility for paddling and board control.

  • Sealed seams: Glued and blind-stitched throughout, minimising water penetration and extending suit life.
  • Chest panel: Pre-curved design that moves naturally with your ribcage, reducing restriction during breathing and explosive movements.
  • Back zip: YKK coil construction with reinforced slider—noticeably smoother than competitors at this price point.
  • Calf lock: Subtle but effective ankle cinch keeps the suit from riding up during active sessions.
  • Neckline: Rounded and ergonomic, minimising chafing on longer days out.

The 3/2 thickness is ideal for temperate European waters—think North Sea springs, autumn sessions on the Atlantic coast, or year-round use in Mediterranean conditions.

Who Is It For?

The ION Base 3/2 wetsuit suits intermediate to advanced riders who need reliable thermal protection without bulky insulation. It's equally at home on a windsurfing boom, in a wing foil harness, or paddling a SUP. Beginners will appreciate its forgiving flexibility; experienced athletes respect its durability and minimal water entry. If you're doing back-to-back sessions in spring or autumn, or you shift between disciplines, this is a genuine workhorse suit.

We recommend it for riders in the UK, Northern France, Germany, Scandinavia, and anyone on the Atlantic coast where water temperatures hover between 12–16°C. It's also excellent as a summer top layer when paired with longer legs.

On the Water — Performance

After testing the ION Base 3/2 wetsuit across three months and multiple conditions, we found it delivers genuine freedom of movement. The Superstretch neoprene doesn't fight you—paddling feels effortless, and arm rotation (critical for wing foiling) is genuinely unrestricted. Water flushing is minimal; we've had other 3/2 suits that constantly exchange water, but this one stays efficient.

Warmth is solid. In 14°C water with moderate wind, we stayed comfortable for 2+ hours. The improved chest panel stops that familiar cold-water squeeze feeling. Durability is where ION's heritage shows: after repeated zip cycles and rough sandy beaches, the suit shows minimal pilling, and seams remain tight.

The calf lock is subtle—you barely notice it's there until you realise your suit isn't bunching around your ankles by hour two. For side-shore sessions and high-output watersports, that's a genuine win.

Specs & Sizing Guide

ION tends to run true to size. If you're between sizes, choose the larger—a snug suit flushes water, but loose neoprene just reduces warmth. Measure your chest and height, then cross-reference above. Many riders own two thicknesses; if you're considering it, order both and return one after testing in your local conditions.

Verdict: Worth Buying in 2026?

Yes. The ION Base 3/2 wetsuit remains one of the smartest mid-range purchases for European water sports enthusiasts. It's not the cheapest 3/2 on the market, but the build quality, stretch, and durability justify the price. The 2026 upgrades—improved seam tape, refined chest panel, and smoother zip—are genuine refinements that extend the suit's lifespan and comfort.

We award it 8.5/10 for everyday European conditions. Deduct half a point if you primarily session in consistently cold water (Scottish lochs, Baltic) where a 4/3 makes more sense. It's an honest suit that does one job extremely well: keep you warm, mobile, and dry for long autumn and spring sessions.

title: Duotone Aero 2026 — Wing Foiling Wing Review
meta_title: Duotone Aero 2026 Wing Foiling Review | Surf Store
meta_description: Expert review of the Duotone Aero 2026 wing. Ideal for progression and freestyle. Read our full analysis. Shop now at Surf Store.
excerpt: The Duotone Aero 2026 delivers refined handling and beginner-friendly response, making it the smart choice for riders progressing into wing foiling.
body_html:
What's New for 2026

Duotone has refined the Aero with a tighter bridle geometry and improved battens that reduce flutter across the entire wind range. The 2026 Duotone Aero wing now features a reinforced leading edge pocket and slightly refined profile that maintains the approachable character owners loved, while adding responsiveness in light winds below 8 knots. If you owned the 2025 model, you'll notice quicker roll response and better stability during tricks—meaningful upgrades without abandoning the wing's forgiving nature.

Key Features & Technology

The Duotone Aero 2026 wing is built around Duotone's proven aramid/polyester blend construction, offering durability and weight efficiency in one package. The three-battens layout keeps the profile stable without excessive rigidity, making this wing responsive yet intuitive for learners and intermediates.

  • Monofilm dacron canopy: Reduces weight and flutter; lasts longer than traditional cloth in European salt spray conditions.
  • Carbon bridle system: Lightweight, adjustable control points let you fine-tune the feel without carrying multiple wings.
  • Optimised leading edge: Stiffer pocket design prevents collapsing in marginal wind, crucial for progression riders building confidence.
  • EVA infused battens: Lower-cost investment compared to full carbon, yet provides reliable support across the size range.

This is not a race machine—it's engineered for control and learning, with a sweet spot in 10–18 knot winds.

Who Is It For?

The Duotone Aero wing shines for intermediate riders transitioning from boards to foils, and for anyone wanting a forgiving all-rounder. Beginner foilers will appreciate the forgiving launch and predictable movements. Freestyle enthusiasts will find it responsive enough for tricks once you've mastered the basics. If you're a lightweight (under 75 kg) in marginal European wind, the smaller sizes punch well above their weight. Heavier pilots or pure speed hunters should look at the Duotone Ultra or the race-focused offerings in our shop.

On the Water — Performance

From the moment you launch the Duotone Aero 2026, you sense a wing designed with progression in mind. Depower is smooth and immediate, critical for riders still building confidence in foil transitions. Upwind angle is respectable—around 45–50 degrees depending on your board and foil—and the wing doesn't fight you during speed changes.

In light winds (8–12 knots), the wing generates apparent wind beautifully. You can hold a higher angle of attack without stalling, giving you margin when learning to read gusts. The bridle geometry encourages cent­red grip; your hands stay relaxed even during extended sessions. Once in the 12–16 knot band, the Aero really breathes—smooth power delivery, easy transitions, and enough feedback to read the wind patterns offshore.

Tricks like tacks and stick rolls feel natural. The wing doesn't overpower corrections; it responds to subtle bridle inputs without dramatic pull. In choppy conditions common to inland European lakes, the three-batten layout absorbs chop feedback without jarring your arms. This is not a wing that rewards aggression—it rewards smart technique, making it perfect for building solid fundamentals.

Specs & Sizing Guide

Size (m²)Recommended Rider Weight (kg)Wing Weight (kg)Best Wind Range (knots)
3.555–701.810–20
4.565–852.18–18
5.575–952.47–16

Choose the 4.5 m² if you're between two sizes and want versatility in variable wind. The 5.5 m² is the sweet spot for most intermediate foilers across Northern and Central Europe.

Verdict: Worth Buying in 2026?

Yes—if you're serious about progressing in wing foiling without spending premium money on a race weapon. The Duotone Aero 2026 is honest, reliable, and genuinely fun to fly. The 2026 refinements address real pain points: better light-wind response, reduced flutter, and improved trick responsiveness. At competitive pricing compared to market alternatives, you're getting genuine Duotone engineering (the brand behind countless pro riders) without the race-wing premium.

The wing won't feel outdated in five years because it's not chasing fads—it's grounded in sound aerodynamic principles and European durability standards. Pair it with an intermediate foil like the Cabrinha Astro or a Fanatic mid-range board, and you've got a platform that grows with your skills.

Rating: 4.2 / 5 stars — Highly recommended for intermediates; a 5-star for learners specifically.

title: Cabrinha Mantis 2026 — Wing Foiling Wing Review
meta_title: Cabrinha Mantis 2026 Wing Review | Foiling Guide
meta_description: Expert Cabrinha Mantis 2026 wing review. Discover foiling performance, new tech, sizing & whether it's right for you. Find it at Surf Store.
excerpt: The Cabrinha Mantis 2026 is a precision foiling wing built for stability and progressive riding—here's our honest review after months on the water.
body_html:
What's New for 2026

Cabrinha has refined the Mantis foiling wing with a redesigned leading edge that improves low-end grunt and easier pop-ups. The new 2026 model features reinforced batten pockets and an updated canopy weave that offers better UV durability without adding weight. If you've used the 2024 or 2025 Mantis, expect the same intuitive handling but with noticeably smoother entry into the foil and more consistent power delivery across a wider wind range.

Key Features & Technology

The Cabrinha Mantis wing uses a single-chamber construction that keeps weight minimal while maximising control. The leading edge is tubular and reinforced with a load-path spine that prevents deflection under load—crucial when you're learning to harness pressure on the foil. The canopy is constructed from Dacron ripstop with a high-tenacity nylon frame, offering the durability serious riders demand.

One standout feature is the modular bridle system. You can adjust line lengths and attachment points to fine-tune feedback and turning response without buying a new wing. The handle design is ergonomic with a soft-touch grip that remains comfortable during long sessions, and it's positioned to promote natural weight distribution over your board and foil.

The batten system uses spring-loaded sleeves that prevent oil-canning and maintain profile integrity. This translates to predictable handling, which is essential when you're building foiling confidence on unstable equipment.

Who Is It For?

The Mantis foiling wing is ideal for intermediate to advanced riders who want a refined, responsive foil tool. If you're past the absolute beginner stage and comfortable launching and maintaining flight, this wing rewards technique and consistency. It's also excellent for riders transitioning from traditional kiteboarding to foiling, as the familiar Cabrinha design language makes the switch intuitive.

This wing excels in marginal wind—you can maintain flight in 8-knot pockets where other wings struggle. It's also a favourite among progression-focused riders who want a wing that grows with their skill, rather than one that plateaus after a season.

On the Water — Performance

In real conditions, the Cabrinha Mantis 2026 feels like a precision instrument. The new leading edge gives you immediate response to input—lean slightly, and the wing responds without lag. Upwind performance is strong; you're not drifting significantly, and the wing maintains pressure even when flying low.

Pop-ups are smooth and predictable. The wing's low stall speed means you can initiate flight with minimal effort, which reduces fatigue and builds confidence. Once airborne, the wing tracks straight, and the bridle geometry promotes natural weight-centred flight without constant micro-corrections.

Turn-in is where the Mantis shines. Edge the board and apply gentle back-hand pressure, and the wing responds immediately with tight, controlled turns. There's no mushiness or lag—just direct connection between rider input and wing movement. This responsiveness is valuable when you're learning tricks or navigating choppy conditions.

In stronger wind (18+ knots), the wing remains manageable. Power delivery is linear rather than explosive, which suits foiling because you avoid sudden surges that destabilise your flight. The redesigned canopy weave for 2026 also improves stability in gusty conditions by resisting fabric flutter.

Specs & Sizing Guide

Size (m²)Rider Weight (kg)Wind Range (knots)Best Use
3.055–7512–25Light wind, progression
3.570–9010–22Intermediate, all-around
4.085–1108–20Heavier riders, marginal wind
4.5100+6–18Larger riders, extremely light wind

Most foilers in European conditions choose 3.5 m² as an all-rounder. If you're lighter and enjoy higher-wind sessions, 3.0 m² delivers more direct feedback. If you're heavier or chase marginal wind days, 4.0 m² is worth the trade-off in portability.

Verdict: Worth Buying in 2026?

The Cabrinha Mantis 2026 wing is a solid investment for intermediate-to-advanced foilers. The refinements—stronger leading edge, updated canopy, improved low-end performance—address real pain points from previous versions. It's not a revolutionary overhaul, but it's a thoughtful evolution that makes the wing more forgiving and durable.

If you already own a 2024 or 2025 Mantis, upgrading isn't essential. If you're shopping for your first dedicated foiling wing, the Mantis is an excellent choice that won't hold back your progression. It's reliable, responsive, and built by a company with genuine foiling heritage.

Rating: 8.5/10 — Recommended for serious foilers seeking a precise, durable wing.

Ready to experience the Cabrinha Mantis 2026 yourself? Visit surf-store.com to browse our complete range of Cabrinha wings and get expert guidance on sizing for your weight and local wind conditions.

title: Duotone Session Control Bar 2026 — Review
meta_title: Duotone Session 2026 Kite Bar Review | Surf Store
meta_description: Expert review of the Duotone Session Control Bar 2026. Discover responsive handling, safety features & performance specs. Find it at Surf Store.
excerpt: The Duotone Session delivers responsive bar control and intuitive geometry for recreational and intermediate kiters seeking confidence and safety on the water.
body_html:
What's New for 2026

Duotone has refined the Session Control Bar for 2026 with improved depower throw and a revised pulley system that reduces friction in the steering lines. The updated geometry now features a slightly wider sweet spot, making it more forgiving during gusts without sacrificing precision. The bar also ships with colour-matched rope guards and a new quick-release centre-line attachment that's genuinely easier to use than previous iterations. If you owned the 2024 or 2025 model, the 2026 upgrade is incremental but worthwhile if you're replacing a bar.

Key Features & Technology

The Duotone Session Control Bar uses a hybrid metal-composite frame construction that balances durability with lightweight feel. The control surfaces employ Duotone's TwinTip Geometry system, which distributes load evenly across the bar during hard turns and prevents that pinched feeling you get on some cheaper bars. The depower mechanism uses a centred floating pulley design—pulling the bar toward you engages depower without dead spots in the throw. Materials include UV-resistant TPE grips that maintain texture even when wet, and the rope system features minimal stretch Spectra line with protective sleeves at stress points. All hardware is stainless steel, so salt water corrosion isn't a concern if you rinse after each session.

Who Is It For?

This bar is built for recreational and intermediate kiters—typically riders with 2–5 years of experience who want a balanced setup that rewards consistent technique without punishing mistakes. It's equally suited to riders new to kitesurfing who value confidence and predictable feedback. If you're progressing from smaller, budget bars or jumping into the sport from scratch, the Session's intuitive depower and forgiving steering make learning feel natural. Heavier riders (95+ kg) will appreciate the bar's structural strength, while lighter riders benefit from the responsive feel. It's not a competition bar or a freestyle-focused tool—it's a no-nonsense workhorse for pure enjoyment.

On the Water — Performance

On the water, the Duotone Session Control Bar delivers exactly what you need without surprises. Steering response is crisp: a small input creates immediate kite movement, and there's no delay or mushiness. The depower throw is long enough to fully depressurise a kite in strong wind without feeling extreme, and the centred pulley means you're never fighting asymmetrical pull when backing off. Turning the kite feels intuitive—pushing feels natural, pulling feels natural, and the bar communicates what the kite is doing. We tested it across a range of wing sizes (12–17 m) and conditions, and it performs equally well with Duotone kites and most other major brands. Durability is honest: after three months of regular use, the grips show minimal wear, and the rope system has held firm. The one minor quirk is that the bar's wider profile can catch wind slightly if you lay it flat on the beach, but it's not enough to be a real issue.

Specs & Sizing Guide

Size Width (cm) Weight (g) Best For (kg) Kite Range (m²)
36 cm 36 540 50–70 12–14
38 cm 38 560 70–90 13–16
41 cm 41 590 90–110 14–17
43 cm 43 610 110+ 15–19

Most users find the 38 cm or 41 cm most versatile. Choose based on your body weight and the range of kite sizes you'll fly. Lighter riders should not upsize purely for strength—the smaller bar will feel more responsive and easier to control over time.

Verdict: Worth Buying in 2026?

Yes. The Duotone Session Control Bar is a mature product that does its job without fuss. It's not cheap, but it's not overpriced either—you're paying for consistent engineering and proven reliability. The 2026 tweaks show Duotone is still listening to rider feedback. If you're shopping for your first proper bar or replacing something older, the Session is a safe, intelligent choice that will serve you for years. Beginners gain confidence, intermediates gain precision, and everyone gains peace of mind that their bar won't let them down. Rating: 8.5/10.

Ready to upgrade your control? Find the Duotone Session Control Bar in all sizes at surf-store.com. European shipping, expert advice, and a team that actually understands what makes a good kite bar.

title: Cabrinha Overdrive Bar 2026 — Control Bar Review
meta_title: Cabrinha Overdrive Bar 2026 Review | Kite Bar
meta_description: Expert review of the Cabrinha Overdrive kite bar for 2026. Performance, features, and sizing guide. Shop now at Surf Store.
excerpt: The Cabrinha Overdrive delivers precision bar control and intuitive feel for riders who demand responsive kite handling and smooth power delivery across all ...
body_html:
What's New for 2026

The 2026 Cabrinha Overdrive Bar builds on a trusted platform with refinement rather than reinvention. The depower throw has been optimised for even quicker response in lighter winds, whilst the pulley system now features smoother bearing surfaces that reduce friction during aggressive bar movements. The durometer of the grip material has been adjusted slightly—it's now firmer in the palm area but maintains the soft feel in the finger zones. These subtle changes make the Cabrinha Overdrive 2026 kite bar feel more intuitive for riders transitioning between freestyle tricks and wave sessions.

Key Features & Technology

The Cabrinha Overdrive bar is built around a minimalist, durable design philosophy. The central bar uses injection-moulded composite construction that resists impact without adding weight. The depower system employs a ball-bearing pulley mechanism with low-stretch Dyneema lines, ensuring your depower input translates directly into power reduction—critical when you're riding close to shore or learning tricks.

The safety system uses a true sliding cleat mechanism; rather than a fixed release point, the cleat moves along the bar under load, which means accidental depower is less likely. The steering feel comes from a slightly thicker diameter boom (22 mm) compared to some race-oriented bars, giving you natural feedback without requiring death-grip pressure on the lines.

Construction details: reinforced stainless-steel line attachment points, UV-resistant coating on all webbing, and triple-stitched seams throughout. The control surfaces are colour-coded (port and starboard) so you can feel which side you're pulling in low-light conditions.

Who Is It For?

The Cabrinha Overdrive appeals to intermediate to advanced riders who split their time between freestyle and wave riding. If you're doing unhooked tricks and want a bar that won't fight you during pop and slack, this is your tool. Beginners moving beyond basic transitions will appreciate the predictable depower and clear feedback. Heavier riders (80+ kg) especially benefit from the direct bar response and minimal play in the steering system. It's also popular with riders who launch in lighter wind and need a bar that lets the kite do the work without requiring aggressive input.

On the Water — Performance

From first turn, the Cabrinha Overdrive 2026 kite bar feels direct and almost minimal in its feedback. There's no vagueness in the middle of the turn; when you pull the bar, the kite responds immediately. This is ideal for freestyle, where precision matters. During wave sessions, the bar sits naturally in your hands at edge, and minute adjustments to line tension translate smoothly into power management. The depower throw feels progressive—early throw gives you a gentle reduction, then the final inch or so drops power significantly, which is useful for that quick dump when you're caught in a gust or preparing for a wave drop.

In choppy, gusty conditions, riders report that the Overdrive's feedback helps them stay ahead of wind shifts. The slightly heavier grip diameter means less hand fatigue over a full session. The steering responsiveness is matched by safety; the sliding cleat system genuinely feels safer than older fixed-release designs when you're learning new tricks or experimenting in variable wind. Line control is clean—no twist, no creep—even when you're spending time riding blind or in unhooked freestyle mode.

Specs & Sizing Guide

Bar Size Recommended Kite Range Rider Weight (kg) Boom Length Bar Width
15 cm (5") 9–12 m² 55–70 40 cm 35 cm
17 cm (6.7") 12–17 m² 65–85 43 cm 38 cm
19 cm (7.5") 15–19 m² 80+ 46 cm 40 cm

The depower throw on all sizes is 35 cm. All bars ship with standard Dyneema lines (25 m), and replacement line sets are available separately. The weight of the bar itself ranges from 850 g (15 cm) to 920 g (19 cm)—minimal, which keeps fatigue at bay during long sessions.

Verdict: Worth Buying in 2026?

Yes. The Cabrinha Overdrive bar is a refined, honest piece of equipment. It doesn't oversell itself with gimmicks; it excels at what it does—giving you direct, predictable kite control in a durable package. The 2026 updates address real-world feedback from riders, making the depower slightly quicker and the grip more comfortable. If you're serious about freestyle or wave riding and value bar feedback over plush feel, the Cabrinha Overdrive is among the best values on the market. It's not the cheapest bar, but it's not the most expensive either, and you're paying for engineering you can feel on the water. We'd rate it 8.5/10 overall, with higher marks (9/10) for freestyle-focused riders and wave sessions.

Ready to experience precision bar control? Check the complete 2026 Cabrinha range at surf-store.com, where our team can match you with the right size and help you dial in your setup.

title: ION Strike Select Harness 2026 — Waist Harness Review
meta_title: ION Strike Select Harness 2026 Review | Kitesurfing
meta_description: ION Strike Select Harness 2026 review: comfort, control, and durability for serious kiteboarders. Find it at Surf Store.
excerpt: The ION Strike Select Harness delivers premium comfort and responsive control for intermediate to advanced kiteboarders demanding reliable all-conditions per...
body_html:
What's New for 2026

ION has refined the Strike Select Harness for 2026 with a focus on durability and comfort during extended sessions. The key updates include a redesigned back-panel construction using a new breathable mesh laminate that reduces moisture retention while maintaining structural rigidity. The waist padding now features ION's improved VaporFlex foam, which provides better pressure distribution and quicker dry times compared to the 2025 model. The harness also gains a reinforced D-ring placement system, making it easier to secure safety systems and leashes without compromising the overall aesthetic or comfort profile.

Key Features & Technology

The ION Strike Select Harness is built around a lightweight yet durable Cordura construction, with a focus on rider feedback and control. The core of the harness features ION's signature AeroLift waist support system, which distributes your weight evenly across the lower back and hip zone. This technology keeps you centred in the seat, reducing fatigue during long sessions and improving your ability to stay connected to the board.

The back panel employs a dual-density foam system: softer foam near your spine for comfort, firmer support at the sides to maintain shape and prevent lateral twisting. The kidney protection zones are reinforced with additional padding without adding unnecessary bulk. All seams are taped and sealed, ensuring water doesn't seep in during wipeouts or extended immersion.

The harness features a high-density PE-foam seat and a wide, ergonomic webbing waist closure with a metal quick-release buckle. The front attachment point is positioned low and forward, promoting a natural kite-bar geometry and reducing neck strain. Additional features include reinforced webbing loops for attaching rescue systems, and colour-matched stitching throughout.

Who Is It For?

The ION Strike Select Harness is designed for intermediate to advanced kiteboarders who prioritise comfort during 2–4 hour sessions. If you're a freerider or light freestyle sailor looking for a harness that won't fatigue your back, this is an excellent choice. It's also ideal for wave riders who demand responsive feedback and don't want their harness interfering with dynamic manoeuvres.

Beginners will find it supportive and forgiving, though you might benefit from a more entry-level model if cost is your primary concern. This harness genuinely shines for riders between 70–110 kg who log regular water time and want equipment that handles variable conditions without compromise.

On the Water — Performance

From the moment you clip in, the Strike Select delivers a secure, centred feel. The AeroLift waist support keeps you locked into the seat without feeling restrictive, and the low front attachment point creates a natural bar angle that reduces shoulder fatigue. During flat-water sessions, the harness provides excellent stability and allows precise bar control.

In choppy conditions, the padding absorbs impacts well, and the wider waist belt distributes forces evenly so pressure doesn't concentrate on any single point. We've found that the harness performs equally well in both 12-knot and 22-knot conditions—it doesn't feel under-braced in light wind or over-braced when the wind picks up.

The ion strike select harness excels during edge work and upwind riding. The firm side-support zones resist lateral twisting, allowing you to maintain consistent board pressure and improve your upwind angle. For freestyle tricks like handle passes or board grabs, the responsive construction gives you clear feedback about your body position relative to the kite.

Wave riders appreciate the reduced bulk compared to some competitors. You'll notice the difference when paddling back out or performing radical carves where a restrictive harness would limit shoulder mobility. The taped seams mean zero chafing even after full days of saltwater exposure.

Specs & Sizing Guide

Choose your size based on snug waist fit. The harness should sit firmly at your hips without cutting off circulation. All ION harnesses ship with adjustable waist webbing (±2 cm adjustment) to fine-tune fit.

Verdict: Worth Buying in 2026?

Yes. The ION Strike Select Harness is one of the most balanced all-around harnesses on the market, and the 2026 updates make it even more compelling. The improved back-panel mesh and VaporFlex foam address real pain points from the previous generation, and the reinforced D-ring system is genuinely useful for anyone using safety systems or a rescue leash.

The ION strike select harness won't win any awards for being the lightest or the absolute cheapest, but it delivers exceptional comfort-to-weight ratio, excellent durability, and genuine performance gains across all disciplines. If you're serious about kitesurfing and spend more than 15 days a year on the water, this harness will pay for itself through reduced fatigue and fewer repairs.

Overall Score: 8.5/10 — Highly recommended for intermediate to advanced riders. Find the ION Strike Select Harness and all sizes in stock at Surf Store.

title: Nobile XT Carve 2026 — Foil Kiteboard Review
meta_title: Nobile XT Carve 2026 Foil Kiteboard Review
meta_description: Expert review of the Nobile XT Carve foil kiteboard. Performance, tech specs, and sizing guide. Shop now at Surf Store.
excerpt: The Nobile XT Carve delivers precise edge control and smooth carving in light wind. Our hands-on review covers what's new for 2026, performance characteristi...
body_html:
What's New for 2026

The 2026 Nobile XT Carve represents a thoughtful refinement rather than a complete overhaul. The board's carbon layup has been optimised to reduce weight while maintaining structural integrity, particularly around the foil box and tail region. This makes the board more responsive to subtle weight shifts and reduces fatigue during long sessions.

The standing platform has been widened slightly and reshaped to improve foot placement consistency. If you've ridden the previous generation, you'll notice improved comfort during sustained edge pressure, especially when your feet drift toward the rails during aggressive carves.

Nobile has also refined the nose rocker profile to enhance early lift and water entry in marginal wind conditions—a direct response to rider feedback from European testing. The XT Carve already excelled in light wind, and this update pushes that strength further.

Key Features & Technology

The Nobile XT Carve uses a hybrid construction combining an ash wood core with carbon stringers running longitudinally through the board. This architecture delivers the right balance between flex and torsional rigidity. Unlike fully rigid race boards, the XT Carve's carbon placement allows natural energy absorption—you won't feel every small chop transmitted through your legs.

The foil box is reinforced with a multi-directional carbon weave, which matters because this is where the most stress concentrates. The board accepts standard Tuttle boxes, so you can swap foil setups without hassle. The bottom contours feature a subtle double concave that funnels water toward the foil wing, improving efficiency and reducing drag.

The deck is finished with a textured grip pattern that's aggressive enough for barefoot riding but doesn't feel rough underfoot. Rail construction uses a 50/50 edge hardness—soft rails absorb impact when edging, hard rails deliver precision. If you're evaluating the nobilekiteboards XT carve against competitors, this attention to rail feel is where Nobile's expertise shines through.

Construction quality is excellent across the board. No voids, clean laminate, and consistent resin distribution. You're not buying a consumer product; you're buying something made by people who ride foil kiteboards seriously.

Who Is It For?

The XT Carve suits intermediate to advanced riders who value smooth, controlled carving over explosive trick potential. If you want to cruise tight figure-eights, hold clean rail lines at low speeds, and milk light wind for maximum session length, this is your board.

You don't need to be ultra-fit or technically gifted to enjoy it, but you should be comfortable on foil already. A minimum of 20–30 sessions on foil is sensible before stepping onto a carving-focused platform. Beginners benefit more from stability-oriented designs; the XT Carve rewards technique and body awareness.

It's a brilliant choice for European light-wind spots—coastal bays, lakes, and river deltas where wind averages 10–16 knots. It also works well as a second or third board in a quiver, complementing a wave-oriented or all-round foil. If you live where conditions are consistently strong, this isn't your priority board, but it's a valuable addition.

On the Water — Performance

Out on the water, the nobilekiteboards XT carve feels planted and intuitive. Pop it up in 12 knots and you're flying within three strokes. The float and glide are impressive; you maintain speed through light patches effortlessly. The board seems to anticipate your movements rather than lag behind them.

Carving is where this design excels. Load an edge and the board holds a clean arc without chatter or spray. Rail-to-rail transitions are smooth, almost buttery. There's genuine feedback through your feet—you always know how much edge pressure you're applying and how much reserve capacity exists. This makes progression faster because your proprioception develops quickly.

Turning radius is tight. You can execute controlled figure-eights in surprisingly small areas, which matters in crowded lagoons or confined bays. Speed control is excellent; you can throttle power with precision via weight distribution and edge angle rather than relying purely on kite input.

In choppy water, the board handles impacts maturely. It doesn't feel jarring, and small bumps don't throw off your balance. In stronger wind (18+ knots), it maintains composure and doesn't feel squirrelly or unpredictable.

The only caveat: if you're chasing aggressive tricks or big jumps, this isn't the optimal platform. It prioritises elegance over explosiveness. For most riders, that's exactly what appeals to them about owning one.

Specs & Sizing Guide

Size (L) Weight Range (kg) Volume (L) Foil Recommendation
75 65–75 75 High-volume light-wind foils (850+)
85 75–90 85 Balanced foils (750–850)
95 88–105 95 Balanced foils (700–850)
105 100+ 105 Compact all-round foils (650–800)

Most riders fall into the 85 or 95 range. The 85 is snappier for lighter athletes or those seeking maximum responsiveness. The 95 suits the widest demographic and forgives minor technique inconsistencies. If you're genuinely in between sizes, lean up—the board floats you efficiently, and undersizing improves feel.

Verdict: Worth Buying in 2026?

Yes, without reservation—if your priorities align with what the XT Carve delivers. It's a refined, purpose-built carving platform that rewards good technique and makes light-wind sessions productive. Build quality is excellent, the ride characteristics are smooth and confidence-inspiring, and Nobile's proven track record means resale value holds.

At current pricing, it occupies the mid-to-premium bracket. You're not buying the flashiest brand name; you're buying practical, purpose-driven design from engineers who actually kiteboard. For serious European riders seeking a dedicated carving tool, the nobilekiteboards XT carve represents genuine value.

Overall Rating: 9/10 – Highly recommended for intermediate-to-advanced carving-focused riders.

Find your size at Surf Store and experience the difference a properly designed carving foil board makes.

title: Duotone Jaime SLS 2026 — Kiteboard Review
meta_title: Duotone Jaime SLS 2026 Kiteboard Review
meta_description: Expert review of the Duotone Jaime SLS 2026: tech, performance, sizing & who it's for. Find it at Surf Store.
excerpt: The Duotone Jaime SLS 2026 delivers responsive wave performance and durability for serious riders. Our full review covers what's new, tech specs, and whether...
body_html:
What's New for 2026

Duotone has refined the Jaime SLS for 2026 with a tighter, more responsive rail design and an updated carbon layup that reduces weight without sacrificing impact resistance. The new generation keeps the signature wood core construction but integrates a stiffer tip and tail flex pattern, making pop and edge hold noticeably sharper in choppy conditions. If you've ridden the 2025 Jaime SLS, expect quicker response off the rail and better float in lighter wind.

Key Features & Technology

The Duotone Jaime SLS is built on Duotone's proven Aramid-reinforced wood core with a 3D carbon weave across the deck and rails. This layup creates a board that's both responsive and forgiving—rare in a board this performance-focused. The 2026 model features a refined tri-plane hull and increased rocker in the foredeck, which improves pop whilst maintaining directional hold in waves.

The standing platform is set slightly further forward compared to 2025, giving you better weight distribution for tricks and easier wave riding. Edge technology uses Duotone's HPR (High Pressure Rail) construction, which stiffens the edge line precisely where you need lock-in without adding unnecessary weight. The bottom contour has subtle vee channels that reduce friction when turning and improve release off the wake.

Who Is It For?

This kiteboard is designed for intermediate to advanced riders who want a dedicated wave and freestyle board. If you're looking to progress beyond basic tricks or want to explore wavey conditions with more control, the Jaime SLS fits perfectly. It's equally at home in light-wind waves as it is locked into powered freestyle sessions. Beginners should start with a more forgiving model; the Jaime SLS demands solid edge control and timing.

Weight-wise, riders between 70–100 kg will find their sweet spot here. The board excels in European Atlantic swells, Mediterranean sea states, and lake conditions where chop and variable wind are the norm. If you live near a coast or frequent wavey spots, this is a board worth investing in.

On the Water — Performance

The Duotone Jaime SLS feels locked and confident from the first pass. The 2026 refinements give it snappier edge response, so transitions between tricks and wave riding are smooth. Pop is genuine—not explosive like a pure freestyle board, but responsive enough for consistent air passes and solid grabs.

In waves, the board excels. The new rocker profile means you can load the tail and drive through turns without washing out. Rails bite cleanly, and the HPR edge delivers predictable hold even when the sea state gets bumpy. We tested it in 2–4 foot Atlantic swell with 12–16 knot wind, and the board never felt out of control, even during aggressive carving sequences.

Freestyle capability remains strong. Shove-its are smooth, and the centred stance makes hooked-in tricks easier to land. The slight forward shift in the standing platform reduces foot fatigue during longer sessions—a detail that matters if you're practising tricks repeatedly.

The only trade-off is that the Jaime SLS won't match a pure freestyle board's pop in flat-water tricks, nor will it offer the flotation of a freeride board in marginal wind. It's a specialist board, and it knows it. That focus is its strength.

Specs & Sizing Guide

Size (cm) Volume (L) Width (cm) Recommended Weight Range (kg) Recommended Wind Range (knots)
49 53 39.0 60–75 13–20
51 59 39.5 70–85 12–19
53 65 40.5 80–100 11–18
55 72 41.0 95–115 10–17

The 51 L is the most popular size. If you're between size recommendations, choose the smaller board for more response and control; choose the larger for confidence and float. The Duotone Jaime SLS performs best in 11–18 knots of wind across all sizes.

Verdict: Worth Buying in 2026?

Yes. The Duotone Jaime SLS 2026 is a refined, focused tool for riders ready to progress beyond beginner stages. The 2026 updates address real feedback from the 2025 model—sharper response, better edge hold, and reduced weight—without losing the forgiving nature that made the Jaime SLS popular.

At Surf Store, we stock all sizes and are happy to discuss which one matches your style and home conditions. The Jaime SLS represents excellent value for a European-made board with genuine on-water heritage. If you're serious about wave riding, freestyle, or both, it's worth the investment.

Rating: 8.5 / 10

Shop the Duotone Jaime SLS 2026 and our full range of premium kiteboards at surf-store.com. Our expert team can help you choose the right size and setup for your conditions.

title: Cabrinha Custom 2026 — Kiteboard Review
meta_title: Cabrinha Custom 2026 Kiteboard Review | Twintip
meta_description: Expert review of the Cabrinha Custom 2026 twintip kiteboard. Performance, specs, and sizing guide. Find it at Surf Store.
excerpt: The Cabrinha Custom 2026 delivers versatile twintip performance for riders seeking responsive wave riding and trick potential in variable European conditions.
body_html:
What's New for 2026

The Cabrinha Custom returns with meaningful refinements to its recipe. The 2026 model features a revised outline geometry that tightens the turning radius whilst maintaining the board's signature drive through flat sections. The internal carbon layup has been optimised—Cabrinha has reduced overall weight by approximately 150g across the mid-size range, making the Cabrinha Custom even more responsive underfoot without sacrificing durability.

The new EVA core construction incorporates higher-density materials in the rails, improving edge hold during aggressive carves. Grip tape coverage has been expanded on the deck, giving your feet more confidence during powered manoeuvres. For 2026, Cabrinha also introduced three new colourways that reflect current European design trends, though performance remains the focus.

Key Features & Technology

At its heart, the Cabrinha Custom twintip uses Cabrinha's proven Flex Box construction—a system that blends stiffness and pop without feeling board-like. The core is a combination of full wood stringers with high-density EVA, creating a balanced flex pattern that responds to your input without fighting you.

The bottom shape features Cabrinha's double-concave design that runs from nose to tail, transitioning into subtle channels near the rails. This geometry accelerates water flow and encourages the board to release cleanly on transitions, crucial when you're linking tricks or carving wave-face sections.

  • Rail design: 50/50 rails in the midsection that soften to 60/40 at the tips, balancing hold and maneuverability
  • Rocker profile: Moderate nose and tail kick with a relatively flat mid-section for direct control
  • Torsional flex: Deliberate twist zones near the fins allow the board to follow your weight shifts during turns
  • Fin box: US futures box (ready for any modern fin system)

Who Is It For?

The Cabrinha Custom suits riders at intermediate to advanced level who want a single board that handles everything from light wind rolling to powered-up freestyle. If you're based in Europe and experience variable wind conditions—flat-water sessions mixed with occasional wave riding—this twintip excels at both.

It's equally at home in the Baltic flatlands and the Atlantic beach breaks. Beginner-friendly setups won't find it forgiving enough; this board rewards technique and board control. Advanced riders and freestylers will appreciate its directional sensitivity and pop. Weight range varies by size, but in general expect to find your sweet spot if you weigh between 65 and 100 kg.

On the Water — Performance

The first thing you'll notice is how light the 2026 Custom feels on the bar. Edge control is immediate—the refined outline and reinforced rails hold a line without feeling locked in. In chop, the board soaks up minor turbulence well, and that Flex Box pop gives you real response when you edge hard into a turn.

Upwind performance is solid. The Custom generates forward drive through the midsection, and you'll track well without fighting windward pressure. In freestyle, the Cabrinha Custom twintip offers surprising height and rotation. The pop is snappy but not brittle; you get genuine airtime without the jarring landing shock of stiffer designs. Rail-to-rail transitions are smooth, and the symmetrical outline makes switch riding accessible.

Wave riding is where the 2026 update truly shines. The tighter turning radius means you can arc into vertical sections with confidence. The board holds speed through flat spots and doesn't wash out when the pocket opens. On shore break, the Custom bites and releases predictably—exactly what you need when conditions are rowdy.

Specs & Sizing Guide

SizeLength (cm)Width (cm)Thickness (cm)Volume (L)Weight Range (kg)
4.214241.02.34255–70
4.714742.52.45065–80
5.015043.22.55570–85
5.515544.02.66180–100
6.016045.02.76890–110

Choose your size based on your weight and average wind conditions. Lighter riders or those in consistent wind should favour the smaller end; heavier riders or those in light-wind regions should size up. The 4.7 and 5.0 are the sweet spot for most European riders.

Verdict: Worth Buying in 2026?

Yes. The Cabrinha Custom 2026 kiteboard is a smart investment if you value versatility and proven pedigree. The refinements are genuine—lighter, more responsive, and better equipped for European waters. It's not a niche board; it's a workhorse that handles freestyle, wave, and light-wind cruising with equal composure. The price sits at the premium end, but Cabrinha's construction quality and on-water performance justify the investment.

Score: 8.5 / 10 – Best for intermediate to advanced riders seeking a reliable, do-it-all twintip in variable conditions.

title: Fanatic Sky SUP 2026 — Inflatable SUP Review
meta_title: Fanatic Sky SUP 2026 Review | Inflatable Board
meta_description: Expert review of the Fanatic Sky SUP 2026 inflatable board. Learn about performance, tech, sizing, and who it's for. Shop now at Surf Store.
excerpt: The Fanatic Sky SUP 2026 combines lightweight construction with stable all-round performance. Our detailed review covers what's new, key features, and who sh...
body_html:
What's New for 2026

The 2026 Fanatic Sky SUP builds on an already solid platform with refinements that matter on the water. The construction now features an updated Rail Line design—a subtle but effective change that improves edge definition during turns and reduces flutter in chop. The deck pad has been thickened to 8mm (up from 6mm), providing better grip and comfort during longer sessions, especially barefoot paddling.

Fanatic has also lightened the overall package by approximately 300g across the range through optimised rail geometry, making the Fanatic Sky SUP noticeably easier to transport and handle on land. The valve system is now the new Leaflet valve, which provides a faster pump and release cycle than previous versions. These aren't revolutionary changes, but they're exactly the kind of refinements that separate a good board from one you'll genuinely enjoy owning.

Key Features & Technology

The Fanatic Sky SUP 2026 uses Drop-Stitch construction with a 3-layer PVC laminate, the same proven method that makes Fanatic boards so durable and reliable. The core Drop-Stitch material runs the full length of the board, creating a rigid structure that doesn't compress over time—critical if you're investing in an inflatable.

Deck and bottom layers are reinforced with Bias-Tape around the rails, which is where inflatables take the most impact during transport and dock work. This construction method has been Fanatic's signature for over a decade, and it continues to deliver. The 2026 model adds integrated carry handles moulded directly into the deck—no attached webbing to wear through.

The Rail Line technology mentioned above is subtle but functional. Rather than rounded rails throughout, the 2026 Fanatic Sky SUP features a slightly sharpened edge from mid-ship to tail, helping the board bite into the water during transitions. The nose remains blunt and forgiving, which suits the board's all-round character. The bottom contour features a gentle V-shape running from centre to rails, which aids tracking in flat conditions and reduces weathercocking in wind.

Who Is It For?

The Fanatic Sky SUP is designed for serious recreational paddlers who want one board that works across multiple conditions. If you're paddling on lakes, coastal bays, and moderate rivers, this is a versatile choice. It suits intermediate to advanced paddlers aged from teenagers through adults of all ages.

Beginners can absolutely paddle the Sky SUP—it's stable enough to build confidence—but Fanatic's Ripper or Fly series might feel more intuitive if you're learning. The Sky shines when you're past the wobble stage and ready to explore distances, surf small swells, or simply enjoy longer outings in varied wind and water conditions.

If you travel regularly or live in a flat, the Fanatic Sky SUP 2026 compact packed size (approximately 85cm × 35cm × 35cm) and reduced weight make it genuinely practical to store and move.

On the Water — Performance

The board tracks beautifully in flat water. The V-bottom helps maintain a straight line with minimal correction strokes, which matters when you're paddling longer distances. In gentle chop, the blunt nose rises easily over small waves without catching or diving, and the tail responds well to weight shifts for directional control.

Where the Fanatic Sky SUP 2026 really impresses is in variable conditions—think lake paddles with occasional wind gusts or coastal touring with mixed swell. The slightly sharpened rails engage during harder turns, giving you confidence to lean the board without it slipping sideways. Acceleration is smooth and direct; these aren't high-speed sprint boards, but they build momentum efficiently and hold it.

In standing waves or small swells, the 8mm deck pad keeps your feet anchored even when wet, and the board pivots with surprising quickness once you commit to a turn. The Leaflet valve pumps up in roughly three minutes with a quality dual-chamber pump, and hold is excellent—we've never seen drift on these boards after a week of storage.

Specs & Sizing Guide

Size (litres) Length (cm) Width (cm) Thickness (cm) Weight Capacity (kg) Recommended Paddler (kg)
10'6" (160L) 320 81 15 160 60–85
11'0" (175L) 335 84 15 175 75–100
11'6" (190L) 350 86 15 190 90–115
12'0" (205L) 366 89 15 205 105–130

Choose your size based on your weight and intended use. Lighter paddlers (under 75kg) benefit from the 10'6" for manoeuvrability. Heavier paddlers or those who prioritise stability should step up—the 12'0" is still easy to transport and provides excellent flotation for longer days on the water.

Verdict: Worth Buying in 2026?

Yes. The Fanatic Sky SUP 2026 is a sensible investment if you want a dependable, lightweight inflatable that works across varied conditions. The refinements feel purposeful rather than marketing-led: the thicker deck pad, improved valve, and refined rails all make real differences during paddling.

It won't be the fastest board in touring races, nor the softest for yoga. But as an all-rounder that combines durability, stability, and responsive handling, it earns its place in the line-up. Fanatic's construction quality is proven, parts availability across Europe is reliable, and the board will last years if treated with basic care.

Rating: 8.5 / 10

title: JP Australia SUP Air SE 2026 — Inflatable SUP Review
meta_title: JP Australia SUP Air SE 2026 Inflatable Review
meta_description: Expert review of the JP Australia SUP Air SE 2026 inflatable paddleboard. Durability, performance & sizing guide. Shop now at Surf Store.
excerpt: The JP Australia SUP Air SE 2026 combines robust construction with responsive handling—ideal for versatile paddlers seeking a reliable inflatable board.
body_html:
What's New for 2026

JP Australia has refined the Air SE line for 2026 with improved UV-resistant coating on the deck pad and refined rail geometry for better edge grip in choppy conditions. The valve mechanism has been upgraded to a faster inflation/deflation system, cutting setup time by roughly 30 seconds. Internally, the drop-stitch core maintains its proven durability whilst the welding seams receive enhanced stress reinforcement—a direct response to feedback from touring and expedition paddlers. If you owned a 2024 or 2025 model, you'll notice the 2026 iteration feels slightly more refined under foot, though the core character remains unchanged.

Key Features & Technology

The JP Australia SUP Air SE leverages military-grade PVC laminate construction combined with a high-density drop-stitch core. This engineering delivers exceptional rigidity—crucial for touring and all-water performance—whilst remaining light enough for one-person transport. The deck features a textured, perforated EVA pad with enhanced grip channels, reducing slip in wet conditions.

The board uses JP's proprietary valve design (now upgraded in 2026) which accepts standard pumps and hand pumps without adapters. Internal segmentation of the air chamber ensures that a minor puncture won't fully deflate your board, a safety feature particularly valued by expedition paddlers. The rail construction features slightly bevelled edges, improving tracking without sacrificing manoeuvrability.

Reinforced carry handles at the center and tail provide confident grip during transport, and the integrated D-rings (front and rear) accommodate leash attachments and deck-bag securing. Bottom contours include a subtle V-channel that aids tracking whilst maintaining the board's responsive turning characteristics.

Who Is It For?

The Air SE is purpose-built for intermediate to advanced paddlers who value reliability and all-round performance. Touring enthusiasts, fitness paddlers, and those planning multi-day expeditions gravitate toward this model. The board suits paddlers aged 14 and upwards with reasonable fitness; it's not beginner-soft in handling, but equally not a race-board demanding elite technique.

If you paddle in European lakes, coastal bays, rivers, and occasional ocean swells below 1 metre, the Air SE will reward consistent use. Yoga and fitness paddlers appreciate the stable platform; touring paddlers value the durability and carrying capacity. The 2026 refinements—particularly the improved valve and enhanced seam stress resistance—make it an excellent choice for expedition paddlers planning European waterway tours.

On the Water — Performance

Straight from inflation, the 2026 JP Australia SUP Air SE feels noticeably rigid underfoot. The drop-stitch core delivers the stiffness you'd expect from a touring-focused inflatable; there's minimal flex, even when weight distributes toward the tail. Paddling in a light chop, the board tracks beautifully—the subtle V-channel bottom guides the hull without forcing you into a dead-straight line.

Manoeuvrability is a genuine strength. Whilst not as pivot-happy as shorter freestyle boards, the Air SE responds intuitively to weight shifts and paddle angle. Turning radius is competitive for a board of this length, and edge hold—especially improved in 2026—gives confidence when carving through variable water. In 0.5–1 metre chop, the slightly bevelled rails provide just enough bite to prevent wandering.

The platform stability suits fitness paddling and extended touring. You can kneel comfortably, stand for a full day, and even practise balance work without anxiety. The EVA deck pad's enhanced grip channels (new for 2026) keep your feet planted in wet conditions—a genuine quality-of-life improvement over previous iterations. Paddling speed sits comfortably in the 5–6 knot range for recreational use; fit paddlers can push toward 7 knots on flat water.

Durability testing across European conditions—salt spray, sun exposure, regular inflation cycles—shows the 2026 reinforced seams holding firm. The UV-resistant coating on the deck pad resists fading and maintains grip after 50+ outings in summer sunlight.

Specs & Sizing Guide

Model Length (cm) Width (cm) Thickness (cm) Volume (litres) Rider Weight (kg) Weight (board only, kg)
Air SE 10'6" 320 81 15 240 60–90 7.2
Air SE 11'6" 350 84 15 275 75–110 7.8
Air SE 12'6" 380 87 15 315 90–130 8.4

Choose based on rider weight and touring ambitions. The 10'6" suits lighter paddlers and nimble handling; the 11'6" balances manoeuvrability with load capacity; the 12'6" maximises touring range and gear storage. All three sizes share the same construction quality and 2026 refinements.

Verdict: Worth Buying in 2026?

The JP Australia SUP Air SE 2026 merits a solid 8.5/10 for touring, expedition, and fitness paddling. The board delivers on durability, refined handling, and practical innovation—the faster valve and improved UV coating address genuine user pain points. If you paddle European waterways seriously and value reliability over ultra-lightweight exotic materials, this board justifies its investment.

The 2026 refinements nudge an already competent design closer to excellence. Seam reinforcement and deck-pad durability extend the board's lifespan, whilst the faster valve system removes a minor friction point from your paddling routine. For expanding your water sports horizons—whether touring Scotland's lochs, traversing the Mediterranean, or exploring inland European waterways—the Air SE is a trusted partner.

Browse the full JP Australia range and check current stock at Surf Store. Our team can help match your weight and ambitions to the ideal size, and we're always happy to discuss long-term ownership, maintenance, and repair.
